news.summary: North Korean hackers associated with the $1.4 billion Bybit exploit are now reportedly targeting crypto developers through fake recruitment tests embedded with malware. The hackers, identified as the hacking group Slow Pisces, lure developers on platforms like LinkedIn with fraudulent job offers and then send coding assignments that contain malware when opened. The hackers aim to steal developer credentials, access codes, cloud configurations, SSH keys, iCloud Keychain, and wallet access. The group, also known as Jade Sleet, Pukchong, TraderTraitor, and UNC4899, use tactics like creating fake employee profiles on professional networking sites to gain access to targeted companies and exploit vulnerabilities. The hackers primarily use platforms like LinkedIn but have been seen on Upwork and Fiverr as well. Cybersecurity experts recommend developers use virtual machines and sandboxes for testing, verify job offers independently, and avoid running code from unknown sources. They also advise against installing unverified packages and stress the importance of using good endpoint protection. It is crucial for developers to be cautious, avoid 'too-good-to-be-true' offers, and reach out to official channels to verify recruiter identities. This scam highlights the need for developer education, operational hygiene, and continuous vigilance in the crypto industry.
news.headline: North Korean hackers target crypto devs with fake tests, linked to $1.4B Bybit exploit. Hackers use LinkedIn and freelance marketplaces to spread malware to unsuspecting devs. Cybersecurity experts warn against fraudulent job offers and advise on best practices to prevent falling victim. Hackers aim to steal credentials and access codes from targeted developers to exploit security gaps.

news.summary: In Tampa, Florida, on April 17, 2025, Better Choice Company, Inc. (NYSE American: BTTR), a pet health and wellness company, revealed its Board of Directors' decision to reinstate the stock repurchase initiative, elevating the authorized amount to $6.5 million for its common stock. Under the preceding $5 million stock repurchase program that terminated on December 31, 2024, the company successfully bought back 102,405 common shares at an average price of $1.9869 per share.

news.summary: On April 17, 2025, the community supporting individuals with Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS) gathered for their annual day of recognition and solidarity in Toronto. Common symptoms include abdominal pain, bloating, constipation, and diarrhea. This day highlighted the importance of supporting those with IBS and working towards better understanding and management of this condition.
news.headline: IBS affects millions globally. World IBS Day on April 19th to raise awareness; Ardelyx the Lead Partner for 2025.

news.summary: Before the opening bell on Monday, April 21, Zions Bancorporation, National Association (NASDAQ: ZION) is set to unveil its first-quarter earnings report. Analysts predict the Salt Lake City, Utah-based company will post earnings of $1.18 per share for the quarter, surpassing the 96 cents per share reported in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be around $794.82 million, an increase from $752 million in the previous year. Wells Fargo analyst Mike Mayo maintained an Equal-Weight rating for Zions Bancorp on March 28 but revised the price target from $62 to $58. The current buzz around Zions Bancorporation has attracted investor attention towards the company's dividends, with the annual dividend yield standing at 3.97%. This translates to a quarterly dividend amount of... More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Zions Bancorp expected to report Q1 earnings of $1.18/share and revenue of $794.82 million, with a dividend yield of 3.97%.

news.summary: The recent listing of Dogecoin via the 21Shares Dogecoin ETP on Swiss exchanges has sparked discussions on whether it is merely a meme coin or a more significant asset. In a recent post on April 16, 21Shares Digital Asset Investment Strategist Max Michielsen argued that Dogecoin is transcending its meme origins, citing its over a decade of existence, strong community, and growing adoption. Michielsen referred to DOGE as "digital folklore" that has translated internet culture into tangible liquidity. Outperforming all top 25 cryptocurrencies in the past decade, Dogecoin has doubled its user base since 2021. Noteworthy features include running on its own blockchain with rapid one-minute blocks. More insights can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Dogecoin's recent success and potential highlighted in new report by 21Shares, outperforming top 25 cryptos over past decade.

news.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump criticized Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell for being "Too Late and Wrong" in his reluctance to cut interest rates, as market forecasts now show a prolonged Fed pause. Trump took to social media to express his frustration, highlighting the European Central Bank's expected 7th interest rate cut. This comes as U.S. market participants, monitored by Polymarket, are heavily betting on no change in the Fed's policy stance for its May 7 meeting. Trump's comments add to the ongoing debate surrounding Fed policies and their alignment with economic conditions.
news.headline: Trump criticizes Fed Chair Powell for not cutting interest rates, while traders predict Fed will maintain rates.

news.summary: In Cape Canaveral, Florida, Sidus Space (NASDAQ: SIDU) revealed their new near real-time vessel detection and classification feature using their hybrid 3D printed LizzieSat® satellite platform. This innovation enhances the Company's capability for efficient detection and classification, providing advanced space mission support.
news.headline: Sidus Space uses LizzieSat® to deliver near real-time maritime intelligence with vessel detection and classification. $SIDU #EdgeAI.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the unemployment rate in the United States has dropped to 4.8%, continuing its downward trend. This marks a significant decrease from the previous rate of 5.2%. The strong job market added 678,000 jobs in the last month, surpassing expectations. Additionally, the labor force participation rate has increased to 61.2%, reflecting more people actively seeking employment. The robust economic recovery has been attributed to various factors, including increased consumer spending and business investments. Overall, the positive data suggests a promising outlook for the labor market and the economy as a whole.
news.headline: Sol-Gel and Mayne Pharma acquire EPSOLAY® and TWYNEO® in the U.S., a deal worth millions.

news.summary: St. Louis-based company Cass Information Systems, Inc. (Nasdaq: CASS) announced their first quarter 2025 earnings, with $0.66 per diluted share, showing growth from $0.52 in the first quarter of 2024 and $0.33 in the fourth quarter of 2024. The net income for this period reached $9.0 million, marking a 25.4% increase from $7.2 million in the same period in 2024 and a substantial $4.4 million spike, equivalent to a 95.2% growth, compared to the fourth quarter of 2024. These results indicate a positive trend for the company.
news.headline: Cass Info Systems Q1 2025: Earnings $0.66/share (up from $0.52 in Q1 2024), Net income $9.0M (+25.4% YoY).

news.summary: Bloomin’ Brands, Inc. will announce its fiscal first quarter results for the period ending March 30, 2025, on May 7, 2025. The announcement is scheduled for 7:00 AM EST, followed by a conference call at 8:30 AM EST on the same day to discuss the financial results. The call will be live streamed on the Company’s website at http://www.bloominbrands.com in the Investors section. A replay of the webcast will also be accessible on the Company’s website after the call. Bloomin’ Brands, Inc. is listed on the Nasdaq as BLMN.
news.headline: Bloomin’ Brands, Inc. will release Q1 results on May 7, 2025, at 7:00 AM EST followed by a conference call at 8:30 AM EST.

news.summary: In March, Super Micro Computer, Inc. has become the most heavily shorted large-cap security amidst escalating worries over U.S. tariffs and tech industry instability. According to Hazeltree's Shortside Crowdedness Report, there has been a significant shift away from tech investments. The report showed a decrease from eight to five in the number of top 10 most crowded shorted large-cap securities from February to March. Hazeltree, a prominent player in active treasury management, highlighted these trends as the industry navigates through increasing uncertainties.
news.headline: Super Micro Computer is the most crowded shorted large-cap security in March, with sector rotation out of tech.

news.summary: In Denver, Spruce Power Holding Corporation (NYSE: SPRU) revealed the appointment of Chris Hayden as Senior Vice President of IT and Enterprise Applications. Hayden, joining the leading distributed solar energy assets company in the US, will enhance Spruce's service platform, expand the Spruce PRO service, and lead innovation in the IT department covering strategy, infrastructure, and application development.
news.headline: Spruce names Chris Hayden as Senior VP of IT and Enterprise Applications, to enhance service platform and drive innovation.
